---
title: Tags & Invalidation
description: Invalidate cached queries by table tag so writes stay consistent.
---

Tag-based invalidation keeps the cache correct without manual key bookkeeping.
The `CacheBridge` tags each cached query by the tables it touches.

## Automatic tagging

```ts
const { rows } = await bridge.query(db, plan); // tagged with plan's tables
```

When you write, the bridge invalidates every cache entry tagged with the
affected table:

```ts
await db.from(orders).insert({ /* ... */ });
await bridge.invalidateAfterWrite(ordersPlan); // drops orders-tagged caches
```

## Manual invalidation

```ts
await bridge.invalidateTag("orders");      // one tag
await bridge.invalidateTags(["orders", "users"]); // several
```

## Why tags?

A single write (e.g. `orders`) can stale many cached queries. Tags let one
invalidation clear them all, keyed by table name — no per-query bookkeeping.

## Best practices

- Let `invalidateAfterWrite` run after every write path.
- Invalidate by table, not by guessing cache keys.
- Use L2 (Redis) tags so all instances see the invalidation.

## Common mistakes

- Forgetting `invalidateAfterWrite` — serves stale data.
- Manual key invalidation that misses some queries.
